title: Feature Request: Support Sandbox Isolation (similar to Docker Sandboxes)
body:
### Feature request description

**Context**

I currently use Podman for my devcontainer setup on Windows with WSL2. The devcontainer runs inside an internal network and communicates only through a proxy container (http/https/tcp). This isolation is critical because I want to run Claude Code with --dangerously-skip-permissions.

After exploring [Docker Sandboxes](https://docs.docker.com/ai/sandboxes), I found them to be even more secure than my current setup. However, switching to Docker Sandboxes introduces some major issues/limitations:
- Proxy limitations: The proxy only supports http/https. Raw TCP communication with the host is not possible. For example, I need the devcontainer to connect to the Android Debug Bridge (adb) server on the Windows host.
- Tooling conflicts: Installing Docker alongside Podman causes significant conflicts. This forces me to choose between ecosystems, even though I prefer Podman.
- DevContainer incompatibility: DevContainer is not currently compatible with Docker Sandboxes. I believe Podman should collaborate with the VSCode team to make this integration work with this feature in Podman.
- Exfiltration concerns: Preventing extraction of information and unintended uploads (e.g., to allowed domains like github.com). I am not sure yet how to control this, but I hope Podman could at least start to investigate this issue for future development. I am not sure if we need new industry‑standard protocols for agent communication :-).

**Proposal**  

It would be extremely valuable if Podman could support a feature similar to [Docker Sandboxes](https://docs.docker.com/ai/sandboxes). This would allow users to benefit from enhanced isolation and security without leaving the Podman ecosystem.

This feature in Podman should not only implement the features of Docker Sandboxes but also solve its current issues/limitations (e.g., missing raw TCP proxy and a secured/controlled way to communicate with the host).

**Impact**

- Maintains compatibility with existing Podman workflows.
- Provides stronger isolation for sensitive use cases (e.g., running AI agents with relaxed permissions).
- Reduces friction for developers who want sandbox‑like security but rely on Podman.

### Suggest potential solution

_No response_

### Have you considered any alternatives?

_No response_

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: @xuanswe thank you for opening the feature request. We don't support such a use case yet, although we already received other requests about that lately cc @mheon @baude @Luap99
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
There is an ongoing discussion on the same topic at https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/28238
